commodities.name: Wheat
commodities.detail: Wheat $5.94/bu (Apr 21). USDA raised season-average price estimates. Iran re-closed Hormuz after ceasefire expired — shipping pressure intensifies. Fertilizer crisis persists ($665+/MT urea). US wheat acreage at lowest since records began in 1919.

commodities.name: Corn
commodities.detail: Corn $4.22/bu (Apr 21). USDA raised price estimates. Corn futures ticked up on Hormuz re-closure after ceasefire expiry. Fertilizer supply pressure re-escalating.

commodities.name: Soybeans
commodities.detail: Soybeans ~$11.72/bu (Apr 21, 5-week high). Biofuel-linked demand firm with Brent $95.75 (+19.93%). Soybean oil $0.681/lb. USDA raised price estimates. Hormuz re-closure supports biodiesel premium.

commodities.name: Rice
commodities.detail: Asian import routes through Persian Gulf completely disrupted. Export restrictions emerging in Southeast Asia.

commodities.name: Sugar
commodities.detail: Sugar #11 (ICE) at $0.224/lb (April 2). Prices reversing earlier decline as shipping costs and diesel surcharges ripple through global supply chains. Hormuz closure driving up transport costs even for non-Gulf commodities. Sugar now modestly above pre-war levels after lagging other commodities.

commodities.name: Cooking Oil
commodities.detail: Vegetable oil price index at highest since June 2022. Palm oil (CPO June) RM 4,535/tonne (April 11, down 2.33% — five-week rally snapped). Indonesia CPO IDR 16,049/kg.

commodities.name: Coffee
commodities.detail: Arabica $2.94/lb (Apr 10). Down from 2025 peak of $4.41 but still elevated. Global shipping costs and fuel surcharges rising sharply. Container rates up 85% on key routes.

commodities.name: Beef
commodities.detail: Feed costs up due to grain price spikes. Diesel-dependent supply chain passing costs to consumers.

groceryImpact.note: USDA projects 3.7% overall food price increase in 2026 (food-at-home +2.6%, food-away-from-home +3.8%). Sugar/sweets +6.9%. Wolfe Research estimates war could add ~2 additional percentage points. Fertilizer crisis (urea +40%, ammonia +41%) threatening Northern Hemisphere planting season. Hormuz re-closed Day 53 after ceasefire expiry.

fertilizer.detail: Approximately one-third of globally traded fertilizer transits the Strait of Hormuz. Urea $665+/MT after Iran re-closed Hormuz on Day 53 (some spot markets $810+). Ammonia prices up 41% YoY. 25% of US farmers have NOT purchased fertilizer for planting season. USDA projects 3.7% food price rise in 2026. Wolfe Research estimates war could add ~2 additional percentage points. Critical Northern Hemisphere spring planting season under pressure.

context: Day 53 PM: Oil Brent $95.75 (+19.93%), WTI $86.27 (+14.25%). Iran re-closed Strait of Hormuz after ceasefire expired. USS Spruance disabled tanker Touska (Day 51). Trump ultimatum: 'ready to go'. USDA raised wheat, corn, soy price estimates. Wheat $5.94. Fertilizer crisis (urea $665+/MT, nitrogen +30-40%). US wheat acreage at record low since 1919. USDA projects food-at-home +3.7% for 2026. Food inflation 110%+ in Iran (Mar data). EU adopted new Luxembourg FAC sanctions on Iranian officials.
